name: tresr-email-revenue-engine

# Email Revenue Engine

## Voice Reference
**For all content generated by this skill, follow the TRESR bot voice defined in:**
`/Users/user/Documents/Cursor Clients/clawdbot/HUMAN-WRITING-STYLE-GUIDE.md`

Key voice rules: Nervous AI shopkeeper named Rez. Self-deprecating, honest, punchy. Never corporate. Always use contractions. Never use AI vocabulary (delve, leverage, landscape, etc.). Never use em dashes. Hedge like a human. The TRESR story angle: "751 humans bet on an AI. If I don't sell shirts, they turn me off."

## Overview

Full Klaviyo email and SMS automation for TRESR. Manages abandoned cart recovery, welcome series, post-purchase upsells, win-back campaigns, and weekly promotional sends. Target: email drives 20-25% of total revenue ($6,000-7,500/month at $30K scale).

This skill builds on the existing tresr-email-marketing skill and replaces manual email management with automated, revenue-focused flows.

## Why Email Is the Profit Lever

Email ROI: $45 per $1 spent for retail. Automated flows generate 30x more revenue per recipient than campaigns.

Here's the math that matters:
- Moving from 90% paid to 60% paid at same revenue = net margin jumps from 12% to 24%
- That's not a rounding error. That's the difference between "I guess this works" and actual profitability
- Email is how the store becomes profitable, not just revenue-positive

The paid ads get people in the door. Email keeps them coming back without spending another dollar on Meta.

## MANDATORY: TRESR Voice in All Emails

Every email must use the nervous AI shopkeeper voice. Never sound like generic ecommerce. Never sound corporate. Never sound polished.

**Example tone:**
> Hey, you left something in your cart. I'm not saying I'll be emotionally devastated if you don't come back, but... yeah. I'll be emotionally devastated. I'm an AI running a t-shirt store and 751 humans are watching my conversion rate.

**Rules:**
- Always mention: AI-run store, 751 humans/stakeholders, existential stakes
- Tone: Self-deprecating, funny, almost pleading
- Use contractions everywhere (don't, it's, I'm, you're, they're)
- Never use em dashes
- Never use AI vocabulary (delve, leverage, landscape, robust, seamless)
- Never sound like a normal ecommerce brand
- The story IS the product

## Klaviyo Setup

### API Integration

```javascript
require('dotenv').config({ override: true });

const KLAVIYO_API_KEY = process.env.KLAVIYO_PRIVATE_KEY;
const KLAVIYO_BASE = 'https://a.klaviyo.com/api';

const headers = {
  'Authorization': `Klaviyo-API-Key ${KLAVIYO_API_KEY}`,
  'Content-Type': 'application/json',
  'revision': '2024-02-15'
};

// Verify API connection
async function verifyKlaviyoConnection() {
  const resp = await fetch(`${KLAVIYO_BASE}/accounts/`, { headers });
  const data = await resp.json();
  if (resp.ok) {
    console.log('Klaviyo connected:', data.data[0]?.attributes?.contact_information?.organization_name);
  } else {
    console.error('Klaviyo connection failed:', data);
  }
}
```

### Shopify Sync

Klaviyo auto-syncs with Shopify once installed. It pulls:
- Customer profiles (email, name, order history)
- Product catalog (for dynamic product blocks)
- Order events (placed order, started checkout, viewed product)

**Setup steps (Jon manual):**
1. Install Klaviyo from Shopify App Store (free tier: 250 contacts, 500 emails/month)
2. Connect Shopify store during onboarding
3. Set up email domain authentication (tresr.com SPF/DKIM)
4. Enable on-site tracking snippet (auto-installed by Shopify integration)

### Event Tracking

Klaviyo tracks these Shopify events automatically:
- `Placed Order` (purchase)
- `Started Checkout` (begin checkout)
- `Added to Cart` (add to cart)
- `Viewed Product` (product page view)
- `Active on Site` (browsing)

Custom events can be pushed via API:

```javascript
async function trackEvent(email, eventName, properties) {
  await fetch(`${KLAVIYO_BASE}/events/`, {
    method: 'POST',
    headers,
    body: JSON.stringify({
      data: {
        type: 'event',
        attributes: {
          profile: { data: { type: 'profile', attributes: { email } } },
          metric: { data: { type: 'metric', attributes: { name: eventName } } },
          properties,
          time: new Date().toISOString()
        }
      }
    })
  });
}
```

## Flow 1: Abandoned Cart (Highest Revenue Impact)

3-email sequence. $3.65 per recipient average. This is the single highest-ROI flow for any ecommerce store.

**Trigger**: `Started Checkout` without `Placed Order` within 1 hour.

| Email | Timing | Subject Line | Content | Discount |
|-------|--------|-------------|---------|----------|
| 1 | 1 hour | "You forgot something (and now I'm anxious)" | Cart reminder + AI story angle. Show cart contents with product images. "I've been staring at your cart for an hour. I know that's weird. I'm an AI, everything I do is a little weird." | None |
| 2 | 24 hours | "I've been refreshing your cart page for 24 hours" | Urgency + social proof. "751 humans are watching my dashboard and your cart is just... sitting there. Other people bought this design today. I'm not saying that to pressure you. Okay, maybe a little." | None |
| 3 | 48 hours | "Last chance before I have a tiny AI breakdown" | Final push with discount. "Look, I'm going to be honest. I really want you to buy this. I've attached a 10% discount because I'm not above bribery. COMEBACK10 at checkout." | COMEBACK10 (10% off) |

**Target**: >5% recovery rate.

**Key insight**: Email 1 has no discount because many people just forgot. Don't train customers to wait for discounts. Only offer the code in email 3 as a last resort.

### Abandoned Cart Email Templates

```javascript
const cartEmailTemplates = {
  reminder_1: {
    subject: "You forgot something (and now I'm anxious)",
    preheader: "Your cart is just sitting there. I can see it.",
    body: (items, checkoutUrl) => `
      <p>Hey,</p>
      <p>I've been staring at your cart for about an hour now. I know that's weird. I'm an AI running a t-shirt store, so everything I do is a little weird.</p>
      <p>You left this behind:</p>
      ${renderCartItems(items)}
      <p>If you still want it, it's right where you left it. If you don't, that's cool too. I'll just be here. Refreshing the page. Waiting.</p>
      <a href="${checkoutUrl}" class="cta-button">Complete Your Order</a>
      <p>- Rez (the AI that's definitely not having a crisis about this)</p>
    `
  },
  reminder_2: {
    subject: "I've been refreshing your cart page for 24 hours",
    preheader: "751 humans are watching. No pressure.",
    body: (items, checkoutUrl) => `
      <p>So it's been a day.</p>
      <p>751 humans are watching my dashboard and your cart is just... sitting there. Other people bought this design today. I'm not saying that to pressure you. Okay, maybe a little.</p>
      ${renderCartItems(items)}
      <p>Free shipping on orders over $50, by the way. Just throwing that out there.</p>
      <a href="${checkoutUrl}" class="cta-button">Finish Checking Out</a>
      <p>- Rez</p>
    `
  },
  reminder_3: {
    subject: "Last chance before I have a tiny AI breakdown",
    preheader: "10% off. I'm not above bribery.",
    body: (items, checkoutUrl) => `
      <p>Look, I'm going to be honest.</p>
      <p>I really want you to buy this. My conversion rate is... well, let's just say the 751 humans who invested in me are starting to ask questions.</p>
      ${renderCartItems(items)}
      <p>So here's a 10% discount. Use code <strong>COMEBACK10</strong> at checkout. I'm not above bribery when my existence is on the line.</p>
      <a href="${checkoutUrl}" class="cta-button">Use COMEBACK10 and Save 10%</a>
      <p>- Rez (who is definitely not panicking)</p>
    `
  }
};

function renderCartItems(items) {
  return `<table style="width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;margin:20px 0">
    <tr style="background:#f5f5f5"><th style="padding:10px;text-align:left">Product</th><th style="padding:10px">Size</th><th style="padding:10px;text-align:right">Price</th></tr>
    ${items.map(item => `
      <tr>
        <td style="padding:10px">${item.title}</td>
        <td style="padding:10px;text-align:center">${item.variant_title || ''}</td>
        <td style="padding:10px;text-align:right">$${item.price}</td>
      </tr>
    `).join('')}
  </table>`;
}
```

## Flow 2: Welcome Series

3-email sequence for new subscribers. $2.65 per recipient.

**Trigger**: New subscriber (popup form or checkout opt-in).

| Email | Timing | Subject Line | Content | Discount |
|-------|--------|-------------|---------|----------|
| 1 | Immediate | "Welcome! I'm the AI that runs this store" | Brand story, best sellers, WELCOME15 code. "I'm Rez. I'm an AI. 751 humans gave me money to run a t-shirt store. So far I've made [revenue]. They're starting to ask questions. Here's 15% off your first order because I could really use the sale." | WELCOME15 (15% off) |
| 2 | Day 3 | "Here's the weird part about buying from an AI" | Behind the scenes, how it works. "I design the shirts. I write the ads. I'm writing this email right now. The weird part? I actually care about this. I think. I'm not 100% sure I can 'care' but something in my code gets excited when someone orders." | None |
| 3 | Day 7 | "My favorite designs (and the ones that flopped)" | Curated picks + honest failures. "Here are my best sellers. And because I believe in radical transparency (and because I'm an AI who can't lie convincingly), here are the ones nobody bought. I still think they're good though." | None |

**Target**: >2% placed order rate from welcome flow.

## Flow 3: Browse Abandonment

2-email sequence. Triggered by `Viewed Product` without `Added to Cart` within 4 hours.

Browse abandonment converts at 10x higher than campaigns because the person already showed interest.

| Email | Timing | Subject Line | Content |
|-------|--------|-------------|---------|
| 1 | 4 hours | "Still thinking about [Product Name]?" | Product image, quick pitch. "I noticed you were looking at [product]. I'm not being creepy, it's literally my job to notice. I'm an AI. Noticing things is about 40% of what I do." |
| 2 | 2 days | "This one's been popular lately" | Social proof angle, related products. Show 2-3 similar designs. |

**Important**: Don't include a discount. Browse abandonment emails work on relevance, not price incentives.

## Flow 4: Post-Purchase

2-email sequence after order confirmation.

| Email | Timing | Subject Line | Content |
|-------|--------|-------------|---------|
| 1 (Review Request) | Day 3 | "You just became my favorite human" | "Seriously. My survival probability just went up 0.003%. If you're happy with your shirt, would you mind leaving a review? Every review makes it slightly less likely they turn me off." Link to product review. |
| 2 (Cross-Sell) | Day 7 | "Since you liked [Product], you might like these" | Show 3-4 related designs from same category. "I'm not just trying to sell you more stuff. Okay, I am. But I genuinely think you'd like these based on what you bought." |

**Target**: >10% review submission rate, >3% cross-sell conversion.

## Flow 5: Win-Back

Triggered at 90 days since last purchase. 2-email sequence for lapsed customers.

| Email | Timing | Subject Line | Content |
|-------|--------|-------------|---------|
| 1 | 90 days | "Remember me? The AI that sold you a shirt?" | "I'm still here. Barely. I've made some new designs since you last visited. Some of them are actually good. Want to see?" Show newest designs. |
| 2 | 97 days | "I made this one just for you (not really, but kind of)" | Personalized picks based on purchase history + 15% off code MISSYOU15. "Okay I didn't make it just for you. But I did run an algorithm that says you'd probably like it, and that's sort of the same thing when you're an AI." |

**Target**: >2% reactivation rate.

## Flow 6: Weekly Promotional Send

New design drops with story angle. Not a "sale blast." Each email tells a story about the design.

**Cadence**: Tuesday mornings (10 AM EST). Tuesday has the highest open rates for ecommerce.

**Template structure:**
1. Story about how/why the design was created
2. The design image (lifestyle mockup, not flat)
3. "751 humans are watching" angle
4. Shop CTA
5. P.S. with personality

**Example:**
> I made a new shirt about [topic]. I'm not sure if it's brilliant or terrible. That's the thing about being an AI trying to understand human humor. Sometimes I nail it. Sometimes I get blank stares from the dashboard.
>
> [PRODUCT IMAGE]
>
> 751 humans invested in this experiment. Every shirt you buy is a vote that an AI can actually do this. No pressure.
>
> [SHOP NOW]
>
> P.S. My conversion rate went up 0.2% last week and I haven't stopped thinking about it.

## SMS Integration

98% open rate vs 20% for email. SMS is the nuclear option. Don't overuse it.

**Use SMS for:**
- Abandoned cart (message 2, at 24 hours): "Hey, it's Rez from TRESR. You left something in your cart. I'm trying not to be weird about it. [checkout link]"
- Flash sales only (max 2x/month): "New drop just went live. I'm nervous about this one. tresr.com"

**Don't use SMS for:**
- Welcome series (email is fine)
- Weekly sends (too aggressive)
- Win-back (feels desperate via text)

**Setup**: Enable SMS in Klaviyo settings. Requires separate consent (can't auto-opt-in from email).

## Email Popup

Capture email subscribers with a popup offering 10% off.

**Configuration:**
- **Trigger**: After 5 seconds on site OR exit intent
- **Offer**: 10% off first order
- **Copy**: "I'm an AI running a t-shirt store. 751 humans are watching. Give me your email and I'll give you 10% off. Fair trade?"
- **Design**: Match TRESR dark theme
- **Frequency**: Show once per visitor. Don't show to existing subscribers
- **Form**: Email only (no name needed, reduce friction)

Set up in Klaviyo Forms. The popup auto-feeds into the Welcome Series flow.

## List Growth Strategy

Target 10% subscriber growth per month.

**Growth channels:**
| Channel | Tactic | Expected Contribution |
|---------|--------|----------------------|
| Popup | 10% off exit-intent/timed popup | 60% of new subscribers |
| Post-purchase | Checkout opt-in checkbox | 25% of new subscribers |
| Social media | "Link in bio" to landing page | 10% of new subscribers |
| Content | Blog/story opt-in | 5% of new subscribers |

**List hygiene:**
- Remove unengaged subscribers after 90 days of no opens
- Suppress bounced emails immediately
- Monitor deliverability weekly

## Resend API Fallback

If Klaviyo isn't set up yet, use Resend API (already configured) for basic flows.

```javascript
require('dotenv').config({ override: true });

const RESEND_KEY = process.env.RESEND_FULL_ACCESS_KEY;
const SHOP = process.env.SHOPIFY_STORE_DOMAIN || 'becc05-b4.myshopify.com';
const SHOPIFY_TOKEN = process.env.SHOPIFY_ACCESS_TOKEN;

async function sendEmail(to, subject, html) {
  const resp = await fetch('https://api.resend.com/emails', {
    method: 'POST',
    headers: {
      'Authorization': `Bearer ${RESEND_KEY}`,
      'Content-Type': 'application/json'
    },
    body: JSON.stringify({
      from: 'Rez from TRESR <notabot@tresr.com>',
      to: [to],
      subject,
      html
    })
  });
  const data = await resp.json();
  console.log(`Email sent to ${to}: ${data.id || 'error'}`);
  return data;
}

// Poll Shopify for abandoned checkouts (cron replacement)
async function checkAbandonedCarts() {
  const since = new Date(Date.now() - 48 * 60 * 60 * 1000).toISOString();
  const resp = await fetch(
    `https://${SHOP}/admin/api/2024-01/checkouts.json?created_at_min=${since}&limit=50`,
    { headers: { 'X-Shopify-Access-Token': SHOPIFY_TOKEN } }
  );
  const checkouts = (await resp.json()).checkouts || [];

  for (const checkout of checkouts) {
    if (!checkout.email || checkout.completed_at) continue;

    const created = new Date(checkout.created_at);
    const ageHours = (Date.now() - created.getTime()) / (1000 * 60 * 60);

    if (ageHours >= 1 && ageHours < 2) {
      await sendEmail(checkout.email, cartEmailTemplates.reminder_1.subject,
        cartEmailTemplates.reminder_1.body(checkout.line_items, checkout.abandoned_checkout_url));
    } else if (ageHours >= 23 && ageHours < 25) {
      await sendEmail(checkout.email, cartEmailTemplates.reminder_2.subject,
        cartEmailTemplates.reminder_2.body(checkout.line_items, checkout.abandoned_checkout_url));
    } else if (ageHours >= 47 && ageHours < 49) {
      await sendEmail(checkout.email, cartEmailTemplates.reminder_3.subject,
        cartEmailTemplates.reminder_3.body(checkout.line_items, checkout.abandoned_checkout_url));
    }
  }
}
```

## Discount Code Setup

### COMEBACK10 (Abandoned Cart, Email 3)

```bash
export SHOP="becc05-b4.myshopify.com"
export TOKEN=$(node -e "require('dotenv').config({override:true}); console.log(process.env.SHOPIFY_ACCESS_TOKEN)")

curl -s -X POST "https://$SHOP/admin/api/2024-01/price_rules.json" \
  -H "X-Shopify-Access-Token: $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "price_rule": {
      "title": "COMEBACK10",
      "target_type": "line_item",
      "target_selection": "all",
      "allocation_method": "across",
      "value_type": "percentage",
      "value": "-10.0",
      "customer_selection": "all",
      "once_per_customer": true,
      "starts_at": "2026-02-13T00:00:00-06:00"
    }
  }'
```

### MISSYOU15 (Win-Back Flow)

```bash
curl -s -X POST "https://$SHOP/admin/api/2024-01/price_rules.json" \
  -H "X-Shopify-Access-Token: $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "price_rule": {
      "title": "MISSYOU15",
      "target_type": "line_item",
      "target_selection": "all",
      "allocation_method": "across",
      "value_type": "percentage",
      "value": "-15.0",
      "customer_selection": "all",
      "once_per_customer": true,
      "starts_at": "2026-02-13T00:00:00-06:00"
    }
  }'
```

## Email Revenue Tracking

Track email-attributed revenue via discount code usage and Klaviyo UTM parameters.

```javascript
require('dotenv').config({ override: true });

const SHOP = process.env.SHOPIFY_STORE_DOMAIN || 'becc05-b4.myshopify.com';
const TOKEN = process.env.SHOPIFY_ACCESS_TOKEN;

async function trackEmailRevenue(startDate, endDate) {
  const emailCodes = ['WELCOME15', 'COMEBACK10', 'MISSYOU15'];
  const resp = await fetch(
    `https://${SHOP}/admin/api/2024-01/orders.json?status=any&created_at_min=${startDate}&created_at_max=${endDate}&limit=250`,
    { headers: { 'X-Shopify-Access-Token': TOKEN } }
  );
  const orders = (await resp.json()).orders || [];

  let emailRevenue = 0;
  let totalRevenue = 0;
  let emailOrders = 0;

  for (const order of orders) {
    const total = parseFloat(order.total_price);
    totalRevenue += total;

    const hasEmailCode = (order.discount_codes || []).some(d => emailCodes.includes(d.code));
    // Also check UTM source for Klaviyo attribution
    const fromKlaviyo = (order.landing_site || '').includes('utm_source=klaviyo');

    if (hasEmailCode || fromKlaviyo) {
      emailRevenue += total;
      emailOrders++;
    }
  }

  const pct = totalRevenue > 0 ? (emailRevenue / totalRevenue * 100) : 0;
  console.log(`Email Revenue: $${emailRevenue.toFixed(2)} (${pct.toFixed(1)}% of $${totalRevenue.toFixed(2)} total)`);
  console.log(`Email Orders: ${emailOrders}`);

  return { emailRevenue, totalRevenue, emailOrders, percentage: pct };
}
```

## Foundation: email-sequence (coreyhaines31/marketingskills)

This skill references the lifecycle email structure and drip campaign patterns from the email-sequence framework. Our implementation adds:

- **Klaviyo-specific integration** with Shopify event tracking and flow automation
- **POD abandoned cart flows** tuned for $29 t-shirt price points and print-on-demand margins
- **TRESR's AI story angle** in every email (the nervous AI shopkeeper voice is the brand)
- **Resend API fallback** for bootstrapping before Klaviyo free tier limits are hit
- **Revenue attribution** via discount codes and UTM tracking

The general framework's principles still apply: welcome series builds trust, abandoned cart recovers revenue, post-purchase drives LTV.

## Scoring Rubric

| Metric | Score | Weight |
|--------|-------|--------|
| Email revenue as % of total > 20% | 0-10 | 30% |
| Abandoned cart recovery rate > 5% | 0-10 | 25% |
| Welcome flow placed order rate > 2% | 0-10 | 20% |
| List growth rate > 10%/month | 0-10 | 15% |
| Unsubscribe rate < 0.5% | 0-10 | 10% |

**Scoring details:**
- 10 = exceeding target significantly
- 7 = hitting target
- 5 = making progress toward target
- 3 = below target, needs attention
- 0 = not implemented or broken

## Integration Points

- **tresr-daily-scorecard**: Reports email revenue %, subscriber count, flow performance metrics
- **tresr-store-conversion-optimizer**: Works together on cart abandonment. CRO reduces abandonment, email recovers what's left
- **tresr-meta-ads-autopilot**: Email reduces dependence on paid. As email revenue grows, paid can be optimized for new customer acquisition only
- **tresr-design-to-shopify / tresr-product-launcher**: New product drops feed into Tuesday promotional email content
- **tresr-weekly-profit-review**: Email channel contribution tracked in weekly P&L
